body.u: I remember walking down this road when going to school I enjoyed those days, especially the summer time. I had taken the secretarial course in the sixth form. I remember I decided this course just a few days before one term started.

We were sent out on work practice for experience in office work. this led to the start of my working life my first interveiwinterview was nerve wracking. It was for a job in some council offices, there were many students applying and I didn’t think I stood a chance, but after a few days I was asked to go back for another interveiwinterview as I had been picked out with about six other people.

They informed me within a week that I had been accepted for the position of shorthand typist. The first day arrived and I went along trying to look confident. the girls were quite friendly towards me and after trial day I was positive I was going to enjoy working there. the work was reasonably well organised so that every girl had an equal amount to do.

I remember receiving my first wage packet. I spent the money on the skirt and shirt I had been saving for. I then opened a bank account and arranged for the rest of my wages to be paid through the bank.

I spent about 2 years working in those offices, and they were quite enjoyable, but the work was not varied and it became boring. I saw a job advertised in a magazine for a receptionist/shorthand typist, I thought that this work might be more varied and that meeting other people would be interesting. they accepted me and I began working there and stayed there for the rest of my working career. I travelled to America twice with the firm after working my way up to personal secretary.
